transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you for calling First National Bank. My name is Victor. Can I take your full name and date of birth for security please?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah, um — hi. So, uh, it's Jenny — Jennifer Bloom. And um, date of birth, it's uh, the fourteenth — no, fifteenth — fifteenth of, um, June. Nineteen ninety.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Ms. Bloom, I've verified your account. How can I help you?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So, uh, my card got declined? Like, twice now. Once at, uh, like a coffee shop and then again at, um — [inaudible] — like a supermarket or something. And I definitely have money, like, I checked the app and my balance is fine.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can see the card has been placed on a temporary fraud hold. There were two purchases in different locations within a short period. Can you confirm — were both of those transactions yours?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Um, yeah — so like, I was at Costa in the morning and then I, uh, went to — uh — Tesco? In the afternoon. So yeah, both mine.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, that would have triggered the security system. I'm lifting the hold now — your card should work immediately.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh brilliant, that's like — yeah, uh, that's a relief. I was starting to like, you know, panic a bit.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Completely understandable. I've also added a geographic travel note for the next seven days to prevent this happening again. Anything else?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: No, that's — that's everything. Um, thanks.
